title: Invalid casts should be avoided
description: Invalid casts should be avoidedAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode SmellcwesuspiciousWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoA cast is anexplicit conversion, which is a way to tell the compiler the intent to convert from one type to another.void Method(object value) { int i; i = (int)value; // Casting (explicit conversion) from float to int }In most cases, the compiler will be able to catch invalid casts between incompatible value types or reference types.However, the compiler will not be able to detect invalid casts tointerfaces.What is the potential impact?Invalid casts will lead to unexpected behaviors or runtime errors such asInvalidCastException.ExceptionsNo issue is reported if the interface has no implementing class in the assembly.Available In:Catch is
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

noncompliant:
// Noncompliant: invalid cast to interface
IMyInterface obj = (IMyInterface)new MyClass(); // Only works if MyClass implements IMyInterface

logic.detection_conditions: Detects explicit cast operations using (Type)expression syntax, especially risky casts from object or to interfaces
logic.triggering_conditions: (Assignment with explicit cast pattern NOT using 'as' or 'is' operators)
logic.reasoning: Invalid casts cause InvalidCastException at runtime. This rule detects explicit casts using (Type) syntax, which are risky when casting from object or to interfaces without runtime type checking. AST-based approach examines Assignment nodes for cast patterns. Excludes safe alternatives like 'is' pattern matching and 'as' operator which handle type mismatches gracefully.
